rulings.Case Title: MARIA ALCIRA LEMUS SANCHEZ VS. TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons By Defendant Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corporation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons By Defendant Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corporation GRANTED. Service of December 29, 2015 is quashed as non-compliant with CCP 146.10. No opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LAMAR WALENT ET AL VS. DANGER PANDA, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT LAMAR WALENT, BRUCE GAULD, MIGUEL SERRA, RYAN BORMAN, REBECCA BORMAN, ANDREAS TARAZI, ANTONIO BRAVO Notice Of Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint Hearing Required. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LARS H ELLNER ET AL VS. PAUL IRACKI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT PAUL IRACKI, PAULA IRACKI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT continued to April 13, 2016. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than March 23, 2016 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES D BANKS VS. THE TESTATE AND INTESTATE SUCCESSORS OF ALINE F. B ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 4. PLAINTIFF JAMES BANKS DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is OVERRULED as to the 11th affirmative defenses; it is sufficiently pled and is not uncertain. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to all other affirmative defenses for defendant to allege facts to support each element of each affirmative defense. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARY NICHOLS VS. MARK STREHLOW ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Complaint For Declaratory Relief, Intentional Interference With Prospective Economic Advantage, And Negligent Interference With Prospective Economic Advantage
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT MARK STREHLOW Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Complaint For Declaratory Relief, Intentional Interference With Prospective Economic Advantage, And Negligent Interference With Prospective Economic Advantage DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden under CCP 425.16's first prong. Tenant's claim for disability is not protected activity within the meaning of CCP 425.16(e)(1) or (4). Tenant's claim is not a conduct in "connection with a public issue or an issue of public interest." Tenant's claim also was not made in "relat[ion] to litigation that is contemplated in good faith and under serious consideration." = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARY NICHOLS VS. MARK STREHLOW ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT MARK STREHLOW DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED. All of Plaintiff's causes of action are premises on her contention that defendant is not disabled. Per DeLaura v. Beckett (2066) 137 Cal.App.4th 542 such challenge must be brought before the Rent Board. Therefore, the demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend and other causes of action are stayed/abated pending the determination of disability. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA LOPEZ VS. CHRISTOPHER R. WILLIAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation And Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 6. PLAINTIFF GLORIA LOPEZ Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation And Settlement Agreement DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show with competent admissible evidence breach of stipulation. = (501/REQ)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 7. DEFENDANT CHARLES MCCULLOUGH DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with 10-day leave to amend for Plaintiff to address the issue raised by demurrer. No opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIANA VANESSA NUNEZ VS. GLORIA GUERRERO NUNEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay The Unlawful Detainer Prooceeding And/Or Motion To Consolidate The Unlawful Proceeding With The Probate Proceeding; Memo Of Points And Auth In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday March 11, 2016, line 8. DEFENDANT GLORIA GUERRERO Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay The Unlawful Detainer Prooceeding And/Or Motion To Consolidate The Unlawful Proceeding With The Probate Proceeding; Memo Of Points And Auth In Support Granted in part and Denied in part. The motion to consolidate is denied. Motion to Stay is granted pending the resolution of probate action. = (501/REQ)
